intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2 (Mexican Mint) are not visible as established specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by a singular, struggling sprout. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Critical Status: Only a single, thin sprout remains near the white rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No evidence of secondary specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Single green sprout present; appears etiolated and fragile.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and damp across all pots, suggesting high moisture retention or recent irrigation.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white granular material (likely crushed eggshells or perlite) in the black pot containing the P3 sprout.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No fungal growth detected, but the lack of vegetative cover indicates a failed or dormant biome.

temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Image quality degradation; visual confirmation of plant life is obscured by low light.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Sprout remains in the same position; no signs of new leaf development or expansion.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total collapse. The singular sprout in P3 is the only remaining biological indicator. The absence of the registered species suggests either total mortality or complete burial/loss of the original specimens.
anomalies: New Introduction/Intervention: White granular debris (eggshells) observed in P3.
Systemic Loss: P1, P2, and P4 are effectively barren.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently failing. The registry provided does not match the visual evidence, which shows mostly empty pots. The only remaining life is a single, etiolated sprout in the black pot (P3). The soil appears consistently damp, which may be contributing to the lack of visible recovery or growth. The presence of white debris suggests a human intervention (likely calcium supplementation via eggshells) that has not yet yielded a positive biological response.
